title: Comanthera jenmanii
intro: Comanthera jenmanii is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Eriocaulaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Comanthera jenmanii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Comanthera jenmanii is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to support respiratory health and immune function.
taxonomy: Comanthera jenmanii is classified as a member of the family Eriocaulaceae and the order Poales. This classification places it within the monocot group of flowering plants.
